summary: Both major issues from round 1 are resolved. _shutdown_proxy no longer accesses mitmproxy internals and now logs errors. The duplication concern is addressed with a valid argument: the 5 remaining unit tests carry the exact names mandated by the TDD anchor; removing them would break spec compliance. Removing the 2 extra non-anchor tests (empty_dir, partial) was the right call. All acceptance criteria are met.

suggestions.suggestion: Integration test still has no @pytest.mark.skipif(sys.platform != 'win32', reason='Windows only'). Patterns.md and task acceptance criteria both indicate Windows-gated intent. Not blocking, but worth adding for CI clarity.
suggestions.benefit: Prevents unexpected failures on Linux CI runners; aligns with project conventions.

suggestions.suggestion: loop_ref / master_ref dict pattern still present. Minor cleanup: use nonlocal in Python 3.12.
